title: gh-131712: Build _suggestions extension on Windows
author: vstinner
body:
Add a test checking that the '_suggestions' extension is available.

<!--
Thanks for your contribution!
Please read this comment in its entirety. It's quite important.

# Pull Request title

It should be in the following format:

```
gh-NNNNN: Summary of the changes made
```

Where: gh-NNNNN refers to the GitHub issue number.

Most PRs will require an issue number. Trivial changes, like fixing a typo, do not need an issue.

# Backport Pull Request title

If this is a backport PR (PR made against branches other than `main`),
please ensure that the PR title is in the following format:

```

```

Where: [X.Y] is the branch name, e.g. [3.6].

GH-NNNN refers to the PR number from `main`.

<!-- gh-issue-number: gh-131712 -->
* Issue: gh-131712

comments.author: vstinner
comments.body:
The code is already built:

```
$ grep _suggestions PCbuild/pythoncore.vcxproj
    <ClCompile Include="..\Modules\_suggestions.c" />
```

This PR only makes the extension available in Python.

comments.author: zooba
comments.body: If we've had any releases with this broken, we should probably add a `NEWS` entry to mention that it's been fixed (describing the user-visible impact, rather than the module itself).

comments.author: vstinner
comments.body:
> If we've had any releases with this broken, we should probably add a NEWS entry to mention that it's been fixed (describing the user-visible impact, rather than the module itself).

I added "skip news" label since there is no impact on users, since there is a fallback implementation in Python: https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/131712#issuecomment-2751287484

comments.author: miss-islington-app[bot]
comments.body:
Thanks @vstinner for the PR 🌮🎉.. I'm working now to backport this PR to: 3.13.
🐍🍒⛏🤖

comments.author: bedevere-app[bot]
comments.body: [GH-131790](https://github.com/python/cpython/pull/131790) is a backport of this pull request to the [3.13 branch](https://github.com/python/cpython/tree/3.13).
